schema: per Cycle-3 brief section 3: workflow_id, step_id, trigger, caller, callee, site, preconditions, ledger/control/artifact/env reads+writes, subprocess, network, lock, durability_point, failure_exceptions, swallowed, retry_owner, retry_limit, idempotency_key, crash_result, recovery_path, operator_signal, terminal_stuck_possible

the_five_phase_shape.note: advance() (pipeline.py:466-546) is the whole engine; every entry point funnels into it.

phases.name: LOCK-FREE producer
phases.work: produce.run_all — whisper / ffmpeg / renders. WARMS artifacts, SAVES NOTHING.

phases.name: TXN #2 (the main reduce)
phases.work: heal_corrupt_gates -> reconcile_source_progress -> source_to_moments -> ingest_moments -> moment_hooks -> hookscore -> [router] -> render_and_caption -> structural_hooks -> refresh_caption_requests -> ingest_captions -> crosspost
phases.rollback: an UNCAUGHT raise rolls back the ENTIRE pass, BY DESIGN (pipeline.py:495-503). Safe because the heavy artifacts were warmed lock-free in phase 3, so the next pass fingerprint-SKIPS onto them.

phases.name: OUT OF LOCK
phases.work: _reconcile_safe (GATED on cfg.is_live_backend, pipeline.py:318) -> _publish_safe (NOT GATED, pipeline.py:334) -> _build_summary + write_digest

the_five_phase_shape.structural_consequence: Step 5's gate asymmetry is the mechanism behind Cycle-2 F-A: reconcile — the ONLY reader of `submitting` — can be switched off while publish keeps CLAIMING posts into `submitting`. Proven by EXP-11.

workflows.name: Daemon tick
workflows.trigger: launchd com.fanops.run (RunAtLoad + KeepAlive; interval rides EnvironmentVariables.FANOPS_DAEMON_INTERVAL, daemon.py:151). There is NO StartInterval. Sibling keeper polls every 120s.

steps.note: side-effect-free; 74 UNCACHED os.getenv calls, re-read per attribute access
RE-READ EVERY TICK — a Studio go_live reaches the daemon within one tick (confirms Cycle-2 SC-2)

steps.callee: except Exception -> print('run halted: ...'); return None

steps.preconditions: s is not None — a HALTED pass writes NO HEARTBEAT

steps.note: no jitter, no backoff on repeated failure

workflows.shutdown: NO signal handler. kill -9 is safe by construction: the run_lease flock and every ledger flock are kernel-released on process death; the SQLite WAL rolls back an open transaction.
workflows.terminal_stuck_possible: C3-F8: a persistent AuthError is re-driven every tick forever, writes no heartbeat, changes no ledger state — and daemon.status still reports `alive` (C3-F9).

workflows.name: Source ingestion -> catalogued Source
workflows.birth_state: pending when cfg.queue_gate is ON (DEFAULT ON), else catalogued (ingest.py:321-323). A `pending` source is INVISIBLE to every pipeline reducer — this is the U4 queue gate, BY DESIGN, not a stall.
workflows.idempotency: TRUE, by content address. add_source is setdefault; Ledger.already_seen(sha256=) is the explicit dedup read. A replay is a NO-OP.

crash_result.after_W2.4_before_W2.5: a staged file with NO ledger row -> rebuild_catalog (ledger.py:746-758) adopts it as SourceState.discovered (matching _SID_RE = ^src_[0-9a-f]{12}$), INERT until an operator promotes it
crash_result.after_W2.5_before_W2.6: the row exists, the inbox copy is not archived -> the next stage_inbox_candidates re-stages it, add_source no-ops. SAFE.

workflows.recovery_path: rebuild_catalog (adds orphans ONLY — never retires a source whose file is missing, ledger.py:746-748)

workflows.name: Source analysis -> moments (the agent-gate machine)
workflows.queue: THE ONLY QUEUE IS A FILESYSTEM QUEUE: 04_agent_io/requests/{kind}__{key}.request.json + .response.json, correlated by a stamped request_id. No broker, no in-process queue.

steps.note: ADOPT-OR-DEFER — NEVER shells whisper under the flock (H10). A cold cache costs ONE TICK of latency, never a held lock.
ATOMIC-PER-SOURCE: `if dec is None: return led` — NOT independent per-persona gates

gate_escalation.moment_hooks / captions: at 3/3 -> a SYNTHESISED clean fail-open response so ingest proceeds (responder.py:159-174)

workflows.stale_answer_guard: _answer_one re-reads latest_request_id after the model call and DROPS the answer if the gate re-seeded mid-call (responder.py:115-119). read_response also compares request_id (agentstep.py:101-102).
workflows.quarantine: every per-unit stage is wrapped; _quarantine flips the unit to its error state via an IMMUTABLE model_copy (pipeline.py:150-152), so one bad source never wedges the pass (F03).
workflows.unanswerable_gate: intro_match is live-wired (intro_match.py:108) but ABSENT from responder._SCHEMA, and answer_pending iterates _SCHEMA only -> it can NEVER be answered. Dormant behind FANOPS_INTRO_TEASE (DEFAULT OFF). Cycle-2 INV-04, UPHELD.
workflows.terminal_stuck_possible: C3-F6 — a torn attempts.json resets the 3-attempt ceiling, making the bounded escalation UNBOUNDED.

workflows.name: Moment decision -> rendered Clip

steps.note: ADOPTS the warm mp4 by fingerprint

steps.idempotency_key: geom = bool(track) or (focus is not None and len(focus) > 2) (clip.py:633); content_type + _REFRAME_GEOM_V (=4) hashed ONLY when geom is true -> centred clips keep their historic fingerprint and never needlessly re-render

steps.note: the .mp4 SUFFIX IS REQUIRED — it picks the muxer (COUP-07 / MOL-78)

workflows.cv2_fail_closed: NEW at fcffa73. With smart_framing ON (the DEFAULT) and the [framing] extra absent, framing.require_cv2 raises ToolchainMissingError -> EXIT 2. This is the ONLY fail-CLOSED optional dependency; every other extra fails open. FANOPS_SMART_FRAMING=0 restores the centre-crop.
workflows.failure_state: a clip that fails to render is BORN ClipState.error (clip.py:830-860) — never laundered into a captioned post with a dangling mp4 (pipeline.py:218)
workflows.known_defect: C3-F12 — framing._detect_faces returns PARTIAL results on an exception (framing.py:151); a crash after 3 of 5 faces is indistinguishable from 'there were 3 faces'.

workflows.name: Caption -> Post minting

steps.ledger_writes: Post(state=awaiting_approval) x owner surfaces; clip -> queued

workflows.caption_scope: the moment OWNER x its platforms, gated by affinity_admits — THE SAME predicate crosspost enforces (pipeline.py:199-206), so caption scope can never drift from post minting.
workflows.post_identity: content-addressed off surface_key = '{account}|{platform}'. add_post is setdefault -> REPLAY IS A NO-OP.
workflows.one_deliberate_exception: _mint_surface_post DELETES and re-mints a post whose prior state is `rejected` or `failed` (crosspost.py:229-231) — so a re-run CAN resurrect a rejected post.
workflows.approval_boundary: every post is born awaiting_approval (models.py:298); publish_due iterates `queued` ONLY (run.py:442); only approve_post — guarded on awaiting_approval (ledger.py:579) — promotes. INV-08 UPHELD.
workflows.note: crosspost.py:286 sets the clip to `queued` UNCONDITIONALLY after the surface loop, EVEN WHEN ZERO POSTS WERE BORN (logged no_post_born).

workflows.name: Approval -> network publication
workflows.note: _publish_one (run.py:242) is the SOLE network-POST caller (INV-09, upheld).

steps.preconditions: state is queued (run.py:266) AND due re-checked UNDER LOCK (run.py:268)
steps.durability_point: TXN EXIT — PERSISTED BEFORE ANY NETWORK I/O (F11)
steps.idempotency_key: the claim itself: a lost race is a clean no-op
steps.crash_result: state = submitting; publish_due NEVER re-drives it

steps.note: *** C3-F1 *** — the post was ALREADY claimed to `submitting` and FINALIZE persists that. _publish_one returns the SUCCESS-SHAPED string 'submitting' after doing NOTHING.

steps.what_it_retries: ONLY _ensure_media (the media UPLOAD). It CANNOT retry the publish POST — see C3-NF1.

steps.network: R2 PUT / Postiz upload / Zernio upload
steps.known_defect: C3-F4 (mkdtemp leak) + C3-F5 (Render.path rewritten INTO the temp dir)

steps.idempotency_key: *** NONE — the backend has NO idempotency key ***
steps.note: this is why needs_reconcile exists as a distinct state

steps.preconditions: submitted AND non-empty public_url -> published (+published_at, publish_hour, publish_dow). EMPTY url -> needs_reconcile.

steps.ledger_writes: merges ONLY _NET_POST_FIELDS (run.py:120) into a FRESHLY LOADED ledger — the B4 lost-update fix. account_id written ONLY when changed ('in-flight wins', run.py:356).

steps.swallowed: everything (fail-open) — a full disk must NEVER roll back a committed live publish

workflows.postiz_permalink_trap: _postiz_permalink ALWAYS returns None BY DESIGN (postiz.py:78-90), so a fresh Postiz publish CANNOT self-promote to `published` — it lands `submitted` with no URL, the promotion gate fails, and it parks in needs_reconcile. THE STEADY-STATE POSTIZ HAPPY PATH IS submitting -> submitted -> needs_reconcile -> published.
workflows.subprocess_in_the_publish_path: publish_due calls postiz_lifecycle.ensure_up(cfg) when `due` is non-empty (run.py:447-449) — it SHELLS DOCKER.
other_entry_points: actions.mark_published (actions.py:280) — operator force, rejects a blank url, WRITES studio_audit.log
cli.cmd_resolve (cli.py:394-398) — the UNGUARDED force-anything escape hatch; SOLE writer of PostState.retired

workflows.name: Reconciliation and metrics
workflows.gating: *** _reconcile_safe is GATED on cfg.is_live_backend (pipeline.py:318) while _publish_safe is NOT (pipeline.py:334). *** When every channel's backend is malformed, live_ready_channels()==[] -> is_live_backend=False -> RECONCILE NEVER RUNS while PUBLISH STILL DOES. Proven by EXP-11.

steps.preconditions: state is submitting AND NO submission_id AND age > _SUBMITTING_HEAL_AFTER (15 min)

steps.note: exceptions are CAPTURED into `results` and re-raised inside the apply, preserving per-post containment
polled_as is a STALE-POLL GUARD: a post whose submission_id changed between poll and apply is skipped (reconcile.py:610-612)

the_terminal_ladder.shape: try: poll() / except AuthError: raise / except Exception: stamp error_reason + CONTINUE (:635) <-- BAILS OUT / then: if published -> promote (:637) / elif failed -> fail (:734) / else (:739): escalation (:746), give-up (:757), `if post.error_reason: continue` (:767), stuck breadcrumb (:772)
the_terminal_ladder.DEFECT_1: C3-F2 — the escalation and give-up sit on the SUCCESSFUL-POLL branch. ZernioStatusClient.get_status RAISES RuntimeError on a 404; PostizStatusClient.get_status RETURNS {'status':'unknown'}. A raising poll continues out at :635 and NEVER reaches :746/:757. THE TERMINAL MACHINERY IS DEAD CODE ON ZERNIO.
the_terminal_ladder.DEFECT_2: C3-F1 — _is_fake_token (reconcile.py:77) gates BOTH :746 AND :757, so a REAL submission_id is structurally excluded from both terminals ON EITHER BACKEND. It gets ONE `stuck ...` breadcrumb, then :767 silently skips it every later pass.

timers_PINNED_correcting_cycle_2.note: BOTH ages are measured from scheduled_time (_parked_age, reconcile.py:61-69), NOT chained. Total time-to-terminal is 72h, NOT 144h as Cycle 2 claimed.

workflows.metrics: track.pull_metrics is the SOLE writer of published -> analyzed (track.py:193). metrics_series is APPEND-ONLY, idempotent per cadence offset. IG reach is read from the Meta Graph (the sole IG metric reader).

workflows.name: Studio mutation
workflows.note: Route->handler->action attribution is DONE (Cycle-2 FIND-013, all 149). This records the DURABILITY semantics of the mutation families.

cross_cutting.authorization: NONE — 0/149 authenticated; all 108 mutating routes CSRF-exposed. Boundary = the network interface (app.run(host=...), default 127.0.0.1:8787). RECORDED DECISION.
cross_cutting.ledger_writes: ALWAYS via Ledger.transaction inside the ACTION, NEVER in the handler
cross_cutting.error_contract: actions return ActionResult(ok, error, detail); handlers render a partial
cross_cutting.browser_sees_success_before_persistence: NO — every mutating action commits its transaction BEFORE constructing its ActionResult
cross_cutting.the_real_ordering_gap: write_audit runs AFTER the transaction commits (actions.py:1008/1040/1070), so a crash between commit and audit LOSES THE AUDIT LINE WHILE KEEPING THE STATE CHANGE. Audit is a RECORD, not a JOURNAL. (C3-F13)
cross_cutting.htmx: an oversize upload re-renders at HTTP 200 with a 'too large' message — htmx 2.x drops non-2xx swaps

idempotency_by_family.requeue x4: state in (failed,error) re-checked under lock; ALL CLEAR submission_id -> safe
idempotency_by_family.publish_now: the CLAIM (state is queued) -> 2nd call is a clean no-op
idempotency_by_family.repost / crosspost: epoch-suffixed id -> a NEW id every click. NOT IDEMPOTENT BY DESIGN ('fan accounts repost freely'). Both mints are awaiting_approval, so no auto-publish risk.
idempotency_by_family.mark_published: rejects a blank url; refuses an already-terminal post (actions.py:274)
idempotency_by_family.bulk_send_to_review: state not in _REVIEW_REVERT_BLOCKED. *** PRESERVES submission_id DELIBERATELY *** -> feeds C3-F1
idempotency_by_family.save_uploads: video-ext check, traversal-safe secure_filename, inbox-bound resolve, ATOMIC .uploadpart -> os.replace, 2 GiB cap
idempotency_by_family.wipe: typed word REMOVE -> MANDATORY verified snapshot -> execute_wipe's own re-check. KNOWN GAP MOL-71: no server-side check that PREVIEW ran.

workflows.execute_wipe: FULLY inside ONE Ledger.transaction (ledger_wipe.py:252-275) -> a raise halfway rolls back ENTIRELY. Gated in code: WipeNotConfirmed unless confirmed; SnapshotRequired unless snapshot_is_restorable — which ACTUALLY OPENS the SQLite file and reads schema_version (ledger_wipe.py:218-235). It writes a .files.txt manifest of media paths but DOES NOT UNLINK MEDIA.

workflows.name: Break-glass and recovery

operations.caller: *** NO PRODUCTION CALLER *** — advertised by ledger_wipe.py:246 as THE wipe rollback path
operations.exclusion: fcntl.flock on ledger.lock — MUTUALLY INVISIBLE to the SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE every other writer uses
operations.verdict: F-B (Cycle 2) — PROVEN silent data loss: a concurrent writer's commit() SUCCEEDS and its data is DISCARDED. Cycle 3 did NOT re-run this destructive path.
SAFE — adds orphans ONLY; never retires a missing-file source
SAFE — the single owner of the recovery transition; refuses a healthy source
T0 reset — purges disk caches, discards gates, reconcile_moments(sid, {})
AUTONOMOUS — but ONLY for posts with NO submission_id
FORCE-ANYTHING; --url required for terminal states; the SOLE writer of PostState.retired
accepts ONLY failed/error — a `submitting` post is NOT OFFERED
rewrites Render.path after a root move

workflows.the_missing_recovery_path: *** There is NO recovery — autonomous or manual — for a post stranded in submitting/needs_reconcile with a REAL submission_id, and NO signal that tells the operator it happened. *** The Studio requeue family accepts only failed/error. The only escape is `fanops resolve <id> failed` then Studio recover — a two-step CLI break-glass requiring the operator to ALREADY KNOW.

workflows.name: Learning, variants, personas, selection
workflows.data_origin: track.pull_metrics is the SOLE writer of Post.metrics / metrics_series / published->analyzed. IG reach: Meta Graph (GraphInsightsClient), the SOLE IG metric reader. Lift = track._W {saves 4.0, shares 4.0, retention 3.0, reach 0.001, likes 0.05}; any weight >= 1.0 is PRIMARY, and a missing primary stamps lift_degraded rather than trusting a partial scalar.

actuators.name: timing_bias
actuators.note: writes an ISOLATED control file; a schedule-slot bias, never a publish

actuators.name: variant_ucb
actuators.note: SELF-DECLARED exception (config.py:821-827) — a scorer swap on the safe caption-bias READ path

actuators.name: adjust.retire
actuators.verdict: *** C3-F10 — THE DESTRUCTIVE ACTUATOR HAS THE WEAKEST GATE. *** The REVERSIBLE bias actuators wait for learning_validated AND >=8 attributed posts x >=2 values (validation_gate.py:52). The guards that DO exist are real: bottom-20% AND lift < lift_floor(20.0) AND not a winner AND NOT lift_degraded (adjust.py:50-52) — which is why this is a HAZARD, not a reachable defect.

inert_reserved_unreachable.verdict: a DEAD GUARD — nothing writes ClipState.published/analyzed, so clip_live is always False
a `return` with NO BODY — the P9 teardown left the call site in place. A live call to a no-op. Harmless.
